mechanism_narrative: The extremely low federal funds rate (0.08%) signals an accommodative monetary policy stance, which reduces the discount rate for future corporate earnings, thereby supporting and boosting S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 20-day forward returns are consistently negative or flat during periods when FEDFUNDS is below 0.25%, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: An elevated unemployment rate (6.7%) indicates significant slack in the labor market, signaling weakening consumer confidence and potential for reduced aggregate demand, which negatively impacts corporate earnings and S&P 500 returns.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 6.7% is above historical averages for healthy economy
failure_condition: If S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive or flat when UNRATE is above 6.5% for two consecutive months, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous weakness in retail sales and housing starts indicates a broad-based contraction in consumer and investment demand, signaling a significant economic downturn that will lead to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive when both RSAFS is below 435000 and HOUST is below 910, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Persistently high consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL 235.64) erodes real corporate earnings and increases the cost of capital, leading to a negative outlook for equity valuations and S&P 500 forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 235.64 indicates significant price levels
failure_condition: If S&P 500 60-day forward returns are consistently positive during periods of CPIAUCSL above 230, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A robust level of non-farm payrolls (137.9 million) signifies a strong and expanding labor market, which underpins consumer spending and corporate revenue growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 137.9M is a high level of employment
failure_condition: If S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative or flat when PAYEMS is consistently above 137000, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A healthy level of industrial production (101.6) reflects robust manufacturing activity and supply-side capacity, indicating a strong economic foundation that supports corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 101.6 suggests stable industrial activity
failure_condition: If S&P 500 5-day forward returns are consistently negative when INDPRO is above 100, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of extremely low federal funds rate (0.08%) and elevated inflation (CPIAUCSL 235.64) suggests a potential policy dilemma or future tightening, creating uncertainty and leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 60-day forward returns are consistently positive when FEDFUNDS is below 0.25% and CPIAUCSL is above 230, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Robust retail sales (433.9 billion) indicate strong consumer confidence and spending, which directly translates into higher corporate revenues and profits, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 433.9B indicates strong consumer spending
failure_condition: If S&P 500 5-day forward returns are consistently negative or flat when RSAFS is above 430000, the hypothesis is rejected.
